commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a direct Hostinger Linux SSH-lease provider with config/env/flag wiring, provider registration, stop-only release and cleanup docs, and fake API regression tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new provider feature rather than a report of broken existing behavior. The relevant verification is real Hostinger lifecycle proof, and the PR currently has only read-only success plus a failed paid purchase attempt.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer needs to decide whether successful paid Hostinger lifecycle proof is required before merge or whether deferred mutation proof is acceptable for the documented stop-only billing contract.

target.title: feat(provider): add Hostinger VPS leases
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a Hostinger VPS provider for Linux SSH leases, including provider registration, config/flag wiring, token-safe API client behavior, docs, and tests.

Safety semantics:
- Hostinger purchases require explicit opt-in with `--hostinger-allow-purchase` or `hostinger.allowPurchase=true`.
- API tokens are accepted from environment/config only and are not exposed as CLI flags.
- Release is stop-based: Crabbox stops the VPS and removes the local claim, but Hostinger billing/subscription ownership remains account-owned.
- Cleanup is conservative: it only acts on canonical Crabbox Hostinger hostnames with local claim labels that the shared cleanup gate marks expired or failed.
- No delete/cancel subscription behavior is claimed.

Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/213

## Verification

Passed locally:
- `gofmt -w $(git ls-files '*.go')`
- `git diff --check`
- `go test ./internal/providers/hostinger ./internal/providers/all ./internal/cli`
- `go test -race ./...`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- `bash scripts/check-docs.sh`
- `npm ci --prefix worker`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`
- `npm run build --prefix worker`
- `bin/crabbox providers --json`
- `bin/crabbox doctor --provider hostinger`
- `~/.codex/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Review follow-up:
- Addressed https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/216#issuecomment-4636085611 by keeping Hostinger catalog item IDs as string identifiers, serializing setup template/data-center IDs as numeric JSON fields, and adding request-body/non-numeric-ID regression coverage.
- Addressed follow-up review on resolved lease reuse by restoring the stored per-lease SSH key for resolved Hostinger targets.
- Addressed updated review on Hostinger setup defaults by explicitly sending `enable_backups:false` and asserting it in request-body coverage.

## Live Hostinger Proof

Classification: read-only proof passed.

High-level read-only evidence:
- `crabbox doctor --provider hostinger` reported Hostinger auth/control-plane/inventory ready with `mutation=false`, `leases=0`, `purchase=explicit`, and `release=stop`.
- Read-only Hostinger API probes succeeded for VPS data centers, templates, public keys, and VM inventory.
- The visible account VM inventory returned zero VPS instances.
- OPTIONS checks showed VM create/setup/stop are POST actions; no mutating action was invoked.

Mutating smoke classification: `external_user_owned`.

Reason: this run did not include explicit approval for billable Hostinger VPS purchase/setup/start/stop/restart/recreate/cancel/delete actions, so no mutating Hostinger smoke was run.

## Config And Secret Notes

- Requires `HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN` or `CRABBOX_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN`.
- The token was loaded locally only for read-only proof and was not committed or included in this PR.
- Required purchase config remains explicit: item id, template id, data center id, and purchase opt-in.
## Hostinger live lifecycle proof attempt (June 5, 2026)

Redacted local proof was attempted on this branch after the latest provider fixes. Token loading used `HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN` from a private local file inside the process environment; the token was not passed as a CLI flag and was not included in public output.

Commands run with `bin/crabbox` built from this branch:

```sh
bin/crabbox doctor --provider hostinger
bin/crabbox warmup --provider hostinger --hostinger-item-id hostingercom-vps-kvm1 --hostinger-template-id 1002 --hostinger-data-center-id 13 --hostinger-allow-purchase --slug proof162451 --ttl 30m --idle-timeout 10m
bin/crabbox warmup --provider hostinger --hostinger-item-id hostingercom-vps-kvm1-usd-1m --hostinger-template-id 1002 --hostinger-data-center-id 13 --hostinger-allow-purchase --slug proof162804 --ttl 30m --idle-timeout 10m
bin/crabbox list --provider hostinger --json --refresh
```

Results:

- Read-only doctor passed: `auth=ready control_plane=ready inventory=ready api=list mutation=false leases=0 runtime=unchecked purchase=explicit release=stop`.
- The parent catalog family id `hostingercom-vps-kvm1` failed before creating a VM with Hostinger `422 [Billing:2004] Item price not found`; this is now clarified in docs/help by commit `8a7cb78`.
- The priced item id `hostingercom-vps-kvm1-usd-1m` reached the purchase endpoint but Hostinger returned `402 Payment Required` with message `Card payment could not be completed, use other payment method or try again`.
- Post-attempt inventory remained empty: `bin/crabbox list --provider hostinger --json --refresh` returned `[]`. No disposable VPS was created, so there was no lease available for run/SSH/stop proof.

Status: the provider code is ready for a real warmup/run/stop smoke, but full billable lifecycle proof is externally blocked by the Hostinger account payment method. A maintainer still needs either a successful paid lifecycle run from an account that can complete purchase, or explicit acceptance that billable mutation proof is deferred.
target.author: coygeek
